name: imodels (Interpretable ML)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Chandan Singh / UC Berkeley

performance.notes: sklearn-compatible; training speed varies by model complexity and sparsity

agent_readiness.notes: Collection of sklearn-compatible interpretable machine learning models. Models: Optimal Classification Trees (GOSDT), Rule-based (Greedy Rule Lists, BRL), Bayesian Rule Lists, RuleFit, SLIM, Sparse Linear, Skope Rules, FIGS (Fast Interpretable Greedy-Tree Sums). All models are interpretable by design — can be inspected as rules or equations. Healthcare, legal, and safety-critical ML where transparency required. Compete with black-box models on small datasets.

gotchas: Interpretable models trade some accuracy for transparency
Best for small datasets — scalability limited
Research-oriented API — less polished than mainstream sklearn
best_when: Healthcare, finance, legal ML requiring model transparency
Small datasets where rule-based models can match black-box accuracy
avoid_when: When accuracy > interpretability (use XGBoost + SHAP instead)
Real-time serving with low latency requirements
summary: imodels provides sklearn-compatible interpretable ML models — Optimal Trees, Greedy Rule Lists, Bayesian Rule Lists, RuleFit, FIGS, and Sparse Linear models. All models are interpretable by design (no post-hoc explanation needed). For healthcare, legal, and safety-critical ML where transparency is required. Competitive with black-box models on small datasets. Research-oriented but production-usable.
